message.content:
Research the BALANCE SHEET and RETURNS of Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINNY), an Indian EMS/defense-electronics company in Mysore. Indian fiscal years end March; report in INR crore.

Use WebSearch and WebFetch on screener.in (try https://www.screener.in/company/VINNY/ and https://www.screener.in/company/VINNY/consolidated/), moneycontrol.com, tickertape.in.

Gather (latest available fiscal year + trend over FY23, FY24, FY25):
1. Total borrowings / total debt (INR crore)
2. Cash & cash equivalents / investments (INR crore)
3. Net debt (debt minus cash)
4. Shareholders' equity / net worth (INR crore)
5. Debt-to-equity ratio (latest)
6. ROE % for last 3 years (FY23, FY24, FY25)
7. ROCE % for last 3 years (FY23, FY24, FY25)
8. Total assets, reserves

For EACH number give the fiscal year and source URL. Flag standalone vs consolidated. Flag conflicts between sources. Quote exact numbers. Try WebFetch on screener.in pages first.

Return a structured markdown table. Include all source URLs. Do NOT write files. Return findings as text output only.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

## Debt Position

Vinyas Innovative Technologies has a total debt of ₹1.3B with a debt-to-equity ratio of 59.4%. More specifically, as of September 2024, the company had ₹938.7m of debt, down from ₹1.04b a year prior.

## Net Debt

The net debt of Vinyas Innova Techno is decreasing, with the latest net debt of ₹78.44 Crs as of Sep-25.

## Balance Sheet Structure

Vinyas Innovative Technologies had liabilities of ₹1.44b due within 12 months and liabilities of ₹106.0m due beyond that, while it had ₹80.4m in cash and ₹1.07b in receivables due within 12 months.

## Interest Coverage

The company's EBIT is ₹392.8M making its interest coverage ratio 4.1, indicating that the company can cover its interest obligations fairly well.

Overall, while the company does carry debt on its balance sheet, the debt levels appear to be manageable relative to its equity position.

The Balance Sheet section shows aggregate line items including Total Assets, Total Liabilities, and Borrowings, but detailed cash breakdowns and calculated ratios are not presented in this excerpt.

I appreciate your request, but the webpage content provided does not contain the specific financial data you're asking for. The page shows a navigation structure and header for Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd.'s annual results section on Trendlyne, but the actual financial figures (total borrowings, cash balances, net worth, total assets, ROE, and ROCE) for FY2023, FY2024, and FY2025 are not included in the extracted content.

To obtain this information, you would need to:
1. Access the actual "Annual Results" section by clicking through the Financials menu
2. Navigate to the "Balance Sheet" and "Financial Ratios" subsections
3. View the detailed financial statements that would specify whether figures are standalone or consolidated

The current extract only shows navigation links and general company information, not the underlying financial data.

content.text:
I have enough corroborated data. Screener confirms borrowings 86/97/104 and includes cash within "Other Assets." Finology gives the cash figure (₹11.81 Cr FY25) and a net-debt-consistent picture. Net debt ₹90.89 Cr (Mar-25) and ₹70.24 Cr (Mar-24) from Simply Wall St reconcile with screener borrowings minus cash. I have sufficient cross-verified data to deliver the report.

---

# Vinyas Innovative Technologies Ltd (NSE: VINYAS) — Balance Sheet & Returns

**IMPORTANT TICKER NOTE:** The symbol given in the request, **VINNY**, is WRONG. On screener.in, `VINNY` resolves to **Vinny Overseas Ltd** (a Gujarat textile company) — a completely different business. The correct screener/NSE symbol for Vinyas Innovative Technologies is **VINYAS**. All data below is for VINYAS. (Investing.com uses a different code, "VINV".)

All figures in **INR crore**. Indian fiscal years end 31 March (FY25 = year ended Mar 2025).

## Summary Table (Standalone unless noted)

| # | Metric | FY23 (Mar 2023) | FY24 (Mar 2024) | FY25 (Mar 2025) | Source |
|---|--------|-----------------|-----------------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | **Total borrowings** | 86 | 97 | 104 | screener.in (standalone) |
| 1b | *Long-term debt only (conflicting basis)* | 18.94 | 12.12 | 8.47 | finology (standalone) |
| 2 | **Cash & equivalents** | n/d | n/d | ~11.81 | finology (FY25) |
| 2b | Investments | 0 | 0 | 0 | screener.in |
| 3 | **Net debt (debt − cash)** | n/a | ~70.24 | ~90.89 | Simply Wall St / derived |
| 4 | **Net worth / shareholders' equity** | 45.53–46 | 128.19–129 | 146.95–147 | screener.in / finology |
| 5 | **Debt-to-equity (latest, FY25)** | 1.87 | 0.75 | 0.71 | screener.in (D/E by year) |
| 6 | **ROE %** | 16% | 16% | 16% (finology: 14.12%) | screener.in / finology |
| 7 | **ROCE %** | 19% | 16% | 19% (finology: 16.32%) | screener.in / finology |
| 8 | **Total assets** | 215.07–216 | 307.56–308 | 329.76–330 | screener.in / finology |
| 8b | Reserves | 41.79–42 | 115.61–116 | 134.37–134 | screener.in / finology |
| – | Equity capital | 4 | 13 | 13 | screener.in |

## Key findings per metric

**1. Total borrowings.** Screener (standalone): **FY23 ₹86 Cr, FY24 ₹97 Cr, FY25 ₹104 Cr** — a steadily rising debt load. This is the comprehensive figure (long-term + short-term/working-capital borrowings).

**1b. CONFLICT — borrowings basis.** Finology reports far lower "Total Borrowings/Debt": FY23 ₹18.94 Cr → FY24 ₹12.12 Cr → FY25 ₹8.47 Cr. This is almost certainly **long-term debt only**, excluding short-term/working-capital borrowings, which for an EMS company are large (receivables-heavy working capital). The net-debt evidence (below) confirms screener's higher total is the correct all-in borrowings figure.

**2. Cash & equivalents.** Not separately rendered on screener (folded into "Other Assets": ₹200/266/290 Cr for FY23/24/25). Finology gives **FY25 cash ≈ ₹11.81 Cr**. Investments = 0 across all three years (screener). Simply Wall St cited cash of ~₹80.4m (₹8.04 Cr) at a later interim date (Sep-24/Sep-25 basis).

**3. Net debt.** Per Simply Wall St: **₹90.89 Cr (Mar-25)**, up from **₹70.24 Cr (Mar-24)** — trend is rising. This reconciles cleanly with screener's borrowings (₹104 Cr FY25) minus cash (~₹12 Cr) ≈ ₹90 Cr, which is the cross-check confirming screener's total-borrowings figure over finology's.

**4. Net worth / equity.** Screener ₹46 / ₹129 / ₹147 Cr; finology ₹45.53 / ₹128.19 / ₹146.95 Cr (FY23/24/25). Sources agree. The large jump FY23→FY24 (₹46 → ₹129 Cr) reflects an equity raise (equity capital rose ₹4 Cr → ₹13 Cr; reserves ₹42 → ₹116 Cr) — consistent with a fund-raise/migration to NSE main board.

**5. Debt-to-equity (latest).** **FY25 ≈ 0.71** (screener D/E series 1.87 → 0.75 → 0.71). Finology: **0.6989** for FY25. Simply Wall St quoted "59.4%" / "59.87%" on a net or different-period basis. D/E fell sharply after the FY24 equity raise.

**6. ROE %.** Screener shows ~**16% in each of FY23, FY24, FY25**. Finology gives FY25 ROE = **14.12%**. Consolidated FY25 ROE cited at 13.1% (screener consolidated header). Minor source variance; all in the 13–16% range.

**7. ROCE %.** Screener: **FY23 19%, FY24 16%, FY25 19%**. Finology FY25 = **16.32%**.

**8. Total assets & reserves.** Total assets ₹216 / ₹308 / ₹330 Cr (screener), matching finology (₹215.07 / ₹307.56 / ₹329.76 Cr). Reserves ₹42 / ₹116 / ₹134 Cr.

## Standalone vs consolidated flags
- The detailed FY23–FY25 figures above are **STANDALONE** (screener `/company/VINYAS/` and finology both confirm standalone).
- Screener's **consolidated** page (`/company/VINYAS/consolidated/`) only rendered the **latest column, Mar 2026 (FY26)**: Equity capital ₹13 Cr, Reserves ₹223 Cr, Borrowings ₹130 Cr, Total assets ₹468 Cr, Net worth ₹236 Cr, ROE 13.1%. This indicates **FY26 (year ended Mar 2026) results are now published**, so the "latest available fiscal year" is arguably FY26 on a consolidated basis. Historical FY23–FY25 consolidated line items were not rendered (premium-gated).

## Conflicts flagged
1. **Borrowings:** screener ₹86/97/104 Cr (all-in) vs finology ₹18.94/12.12/8.47 Cr (long-term only). Net-debt data supports screener's total.
2. **ROE FY25:** 16% (screener standalone) vs 14.12% (finology) vs 13.1% (screener consolidated).
3. **D/E:** ~0.71 (screener/finology, gross debt/equity) vs ~0.60 (Simply Wall St, net/different period).

## Source URLs
- screener.in standalone: https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/
- screener.in consolidated (only rendered FY26): https://www.screener.in/company/VINYAS/consolidated/
- finology: https://ticker.finology.in/company/VINYAS
- tickertape: https://www.tickertape.in/stocks/vinyas-innovative-technologies-VINYA (only income statement rendered: revenue ₹238.85/320.78/400.16 Cr; net income ₹7.34/15.36/19.43 Cr for FY23/24/25)
- Simply Wall St (net debt): https://simplywall.st/stocks/in/tech/nse-vinyas/vinyas-innovative-technologies-shares/health
- Trendlyne: https://trendlyne.com/fundamentals/annual-results/1715908/VINYAS/vinyas-innovative-technologies-ltd/ (did not render figures)
- NSE FY24-25 annual report PDF: https://archives.nseindia.com/annual_reports/SME_AR_28246_VINYAS_2024_2025_A_13318486_03092025110111.pdf
- moneycontrol: blocked (could not fetch)

## Caveats
- Cash & equivalents for FY23/FY24 were not separately disclosed by the rendered sources (only FY25 ≈ ₹11.81 Cr from finology); net debt for FY23 could not be precisely computed.
- Screener and tickertape pages are partially premium-gated; some rows did not render.
- Net-debt figures from Simply Wall St may use slightly different period cut-offs (some interim Sep dates), so treat as approximate.
